description:
I need help setting up a git repository for tracking configuration files on my system. Here's what I need you to do:

1. Initialize a new git repository in the directory `/home/user/config-tracking`

2. Create a configuration file called `app.conf` inside that directory with the following exact content:
```
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
```

3. Add this configuration file to git staging and make an initial commit with the exact commit message: `Initial configuration setup`

4. After completing these steps, create a log file at `/home/user/config-tracking/setup.log` that contains the following information in this exact format:
   - Line 1: The full path to the git repository
   - Line 2: The name of the tracked configuration file
   - Line 3: The commit hash (full 40-character SHA) of the initial commit
   - Line 4: The exact commit message used

Each piece of information should be on its own line with no extra whitespace or formatting. The log file should have exactly 4 lines.
truth:
Directory to be created: /home/user/config-tracking
Git repository location: /home/user/config-tracking/.git

Configuration file: /home/user/config-tracking/app.conf
Configuration file contents (exact):
```
[database]
host=localhost
port=5432
name=production_db

[logging]
level=INFO
path=/var/log/app.log
```

Commit message (exact): Initial configuration setup

Log file location: /home/user/config-tracking/setup.log
Log file format (4 lines):
Line 1: /home/user/config-tracking
Line 2: app.conf
Line 3: <40-character commit SHA - will vary per execution>
Line 4: Initial configuration setup

Verification checks:
1. Directory /home/user/config-tracking exists
2. /home/user/config-tracking/.git directory exists (confirming git init)
3. /home/user/config-tracking/app.conf exists with exact content specified
4. /home/user/config-tracking/setup.log exists with 4 lines
5. Line 1 of setup.log equals "/home/user/config-tracking"
6. Line 2 of setup.log equals "app.conf"
7. Line 3 of setup.log is a valid 40-character hex string (git commit hash)
8. Line 4 of setup.log equals "Initial configuration setup"
9. Running `git log --oneline` in the repository shows exactly one commit
10. The commit hash in setup.log matches the actual commit in the repository
11. The file app.conf is tracked by git (shown in `git ls-files`)

Commands the agent might use:
- mkdir -p /home/user/config-tracking
- cd /home/user/config-tracking
- git init
- cat > app.conf << 'EOF' ... EOF (or echo/printf)
- git add app.conf
- git commit -m "Initial configuration setup"
- git rev-parse HEAD (to get commit hash)
- git log --format=%s -1 (to get commit message)
- Creating setup.log with appropriate content

Pre-task setup required: None (user home directory exists by default)
name: task_000000_bbd7acf3
